key_points: Agriculture provides employment to a large portion of India's population
Primitive subsistence farming uses traditional tools and family labour
Intensive subsistence farming produces maximum output from small land areas
Commercial farming produces crops mainly for the market
Cropping patterns depend on climate, soil, and irrigation
examples: Rice requires high rainfall and temperature above 25°C
Wheat requires cool climate and moderate rainfall
Tea plantations require labour and humid climate
Jute grows well in fertile flood plains
Rubber grows in hot and humid climates
formulas: Crop Distribution = Climate + Soil + Water Availability
Farm Output = Land Resources × Farming Methods
exam_tips: Understand the differences between types of farming
Remember climatic requirements for major crops
Practice explaining why certain crops grow in specific regions
Use examples of crops to support answers in exams
